title: gladiolus imbricatus subsp. crispiflorus
intro: Gladiolus imbricatus subsp. crispiflorus is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the "corky gladiolus" due to its unique corky texture and inflorescence. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and treating skin conditions. The health benefits of Gladiolus imbricatus subsp. crispiflorus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts are typically dried, powdered, and used as a powder or tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
